public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as door warden.
appearance: Typically appears in the bright colors and tall plumes associated with Shelk’s Roadwardens.
manner: Keeps speech clipped and threshold-focused, reading every arrival for risk, weakness, or breach. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a door warden still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of door warden in and around Roadwardens’ Central Command, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Roadwardens’ Central Command workable through the obligations of door warden while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Transpolity.
hidden_pressure: Must notice trouble early enough that force remains legitimate rather than merely reactive. Around Roadwardens’ Central Command, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of door warden visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Any task accepted from Corin Wren may turn into an escort, checkpoint, or route-control problem before the destination is reached.
Corin Wren needs outside hands to handle a breach, seizure, or violent disturbance before it spreads through Roadwardens’ Central Command.

what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Door Warden, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Transpolity.
what_they_refuse: Corin Wren refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.

how_they_can_help: Corin Wren can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Corin Wren adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
